title: gonostegia pentandra subsp. wightii
intro: Gonostegia pentandra subsp. wightii, commonly known as Wight's Gonostegia, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Gonostegia pentandra subsp. wightii include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve its leaves and roots being dried and powdered, then used in various formulations, including decoctions, infusions, and topical creams.
